summary: Focused single-bug retest from iteration_3: verified that the Careers homepage section wraps the language-specific highlight phrase ('JOIN US.' in EN, 'REJOIGNEZ-NOUS.' in FR) inside a `.text-brand-gold` span. Both languages render correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: CareersHeadline helper in /app/frontend/src/components/site/Careers.tsx is locale-agnostic (iterates HIGHLIGHTS for any matching phrase). Works for both fr.json ('REJOIGNEZ-NOUS.') and en.json ('JOIN US.'). Clean, no DRY violations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration_4 retested only the careers-title gold span bug from iteration_3. Verified via Playwright on http://localhost:3000/: EN title -> innerHTML = 'WE WANT TO SHAPE THE FUTURE OF TECHNOLOGY. JOIN US.'; FR title -> innerHTML = 'NOUS VOULONS FAÇONNER L'AVENIR DE LA TECHNOLOGIE. REJOIGNEZ-NOUS.'. Other 78 assertions from iteration_3 were not re-run per request, all were green previously.
rca of the issue: Original bug (iteration_3): inline JSX used `.split('REJOIGNEZ-NOUS.')` which only matched the French phrase, leaving EN title without a gold highlight on 'JOIN US.'. Fix: introduced a `CareersHeadline` helper iterating over HIGHLIGHTS = ['REJOIGNEZ-NOUS.', 'JOIN US.'] and wrapping the matched phrase in a `<span class="text-brand-gold">`. Both locales now produce a single gold span with the correct exact text.
